outlook.headline: GAA remains broadly bullish, but mention momentum is cooling
outlook.summary: The underlying read is still constructive: every company cited GAA as a positive, with Applied Materials seeing expanding adoption and multiple wins, Onto calling the tools critical for process control, ACM seeing strong advanced-node pull, and Ichor benefiting from more process steps. TSMC’s A14 framing also reinforces the long-term need for high-performance, energy-efficient computing. That said, mention intensity has fallen to 0.2x and is down 20% versus trailing average, so the near-term narrative looks more like steady execution than accelerating enthusiasm.

drivers.label: GAA adoption expanding across the supply chain
drivers.detail: Adoption is broadening and generating multiple product wins, indicating healthier near-term demand for enabling equipment.

drivers.label: Tool progress and process-control relevance at customers
drivers.detail: GAA tools are making good progress and remain critical for process control, supporting continued conversion to production use.

drivers.label: Advanced-node cleaning demand remains strong
drivers.detail: GAA is described as a critical application for cleaning technology, pointing to sustained advanced-node pull.

drivers.label: Higher process complexity/content
drivers.detail: More process steps and increased GAA investment are tailwinds for product content and shipment opportunity.

drivers.label: High-performance, energy-efficient compute need persists
drivers.detail: TSMC’s A14 positioning suggests continued structural demand for the most advanced transistor architecture.

risks: Mention momentum is weakening, which could signal slower broadening into more customer commentary or bookings.
Watch whether GAA stays the dominant theme or starts to give way to backside power in upcoming reads.
Customer progress may still be pre-ramp; any delays in qualification or production conversion could flatten the near-term upside.
